body.title: Web UI: message sent from wrong channel tab loses Igor reply
body.result: WS handler now routes to _get_agent_queue('igor') with session_id='comms://igor' regardless of active tab; _deliver_to_tmux also fixed; test added; fanout=0 DROP on device-channel replies eliminated
body.intention: I intend that Igor's web chat window always routes replies back to the sender's visible session, preventing silent drops when the user is in a non-Igor channel tab.
body.description:
When the browser is in a channel tab other than comms://igor (e.g. comms://granny-weatherwax) and the user sends a message, Igor replies to that channel. If the user has since switched tabs, the reply is lost (fanout=0 DROP). Seen in 2026-06-04 logs: DROP session=comms://granny-weatherwax.

Fix options: (a) web UI always sends from comms://igor regardless of active tab, or (b) Igor's reply always goes to comms://igor when msg source=web AND reply_info.session_id is not a user-specific session.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (fallback HTML switchChannel logic), or devices/igor/main.py _process_network_msg

**Design rules:** none apply

**Scope boundary:** Fix the session mismatch; do not change the multi-channel architecture.

**Completion criteria:** Sending a message from the Granny channel tab produces a visible reply in comms://igor; fanout=0 DROP for device-specific channels no longer occurs for Igor replies.
